name: Sun Ra
abstract: Le Sony'r Ra (born Herman Poole Blount, May 22, 1914 – May 30, 1993), better known as Sun Ra, was an American jazz composer, bandleader, piano and synthesizer player, and poet known for his experimental music, "cosmic" philosophy, prolific output, and theatrical performances. For much of his career, Ra led The Arkestra, an ensemble with …

concerts.label: Sun Ra and His Space Arkestra
Sun Ra and His Space Arkestra
Newport Jazz Festival: Sun Ra and His Space Arkestra
Oregon, with Sun Ra, Piano, and the Carla Bley Band
Kool Jazz Festival: Dizzy Gillespie, J.J. Johnson, and Sun Ra

about_quotes.speaker: Marshall Allen
about_quotes.evidence: I was in that. ... When I got with Sun Ra he just broke — tore that stuff away... I don't have to go out and hire people. ... They come when they want — when somebody comes that means the Creator sent somebody to help us out, to do the part.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marshall Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 19, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I played with him a lot in Chicago with the Arkestra and then after I came to New York, he came after that. And I played some gigs with him in New York too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Harrison
about_quotes.evidence: I got there in 1960... I went over to Sun Ra’s house... So I didn’t really eat that much because I was so excited about playing with the group that I’d never played that way before.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Harrison interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 17, 2022 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Harrison
about_quotes.evidence: But meanwhile I would take little gigs with Sun Ra... So they tried me out and so Sun Ra’d say, “Don’t play. I don’t want you to solo. Just play the notes.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Harrison interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 10, 2022 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Smith
about_quotes.evidence: I remember the last time I played with Sun Ra... He said, 'Well I don’t have a flute player today. Oh you play the flute? Get on in here.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, March 17, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I started working with Sun Ra and his Arkestra at a place called the Wonder Inn... I really enjoyed playing with Sun Ra
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
